prompt: What is our true monthly spending — excluding transfers between our own accounts and credit-card payments?

> *Your ongoing spending is about **$6,880 per month**; April’s property-tax payment temporarily pushed the observed average higher.*

Across the three complete months from **April–June 2026**, your true spending was:

| Month | True spending |
|---|---:|
| April | $10,499.77 |
| May | $5,573.99 |
| June | $6,416.90 |
| **3-month average** | **$8,163.55** |

I excluded all transactions categorized as **transfers** and **credit-card payments**, then counted the underlying card purchases once. Refunds reduced spending.

April included a **$3,850 property-tax payment**. Treating that as a separate irregular expense produces a more useful operating baseline of **$6,880.22 per month**. I’d use **$6,880/month for routine planning** and fund property taxes separately.

One limitation: the checking transaction response hit its 50-transaction limit and reached back only to April 9, so April could omit some early-month checking expenses. May and June are the cleaner comparison months.
